mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 5.1% signals a tightening labor market, which correlates with stronger corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances when UNRATE < 5.1 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and disinflation (CPIAUCSL decline) indicates a strong economy with stable pricing, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 50% of instances when both GDPC1 > 16000 and CPIAUCSL < 240.

mechanism_narrative: High housing starts (HOUST) combined with rising industrial production (INDPRO) during late-cycle expansion signals robust demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 40% of instances during late-cycle expansion despite INDPRO > 100 and HOUST > 1100.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% signals monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strong supply-side capacity utilization, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 50% of instances when INDPRO increases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a strong labor market, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 40% of instances when PAYEMS > 140000 and UNRATE < 5.0.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate during a monetary tightening cycle increases borrowing costs, which suppresses equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances during tightening cycles despite the signal.
